attacking_style: Not an attacker; pure sprinter who relies on positioning and timing in bunch finishes rather than initiating moves. Lacks explosive power to attack or follow late accelerations.
team_tactics: Dependent on team positioning for sprint setup. Requires strong lead-out to compensate for lack of top-end power compared to pure fast-twitch sprinters.
terrain_preference: Flat bunch sprints only. Even modest climbs (third-category) expose significant weakness. Cannot contest sprint stages with any meaningful elevation.
vulnerability: Cannot follow wheels of top-tier sprinters (e.g., Kittel) even with perfect position. Dropped immediately on any categorized climbs, even fresh from rest. Pure power deficit limits competitiveness against elite sprinters.
key_patterns: Dropped on even third-category climbs, showing severe climbing limitations (2 observations)
Positioning skills intact but lacks pure power to match elite sprinter acceleration (2 observations)
Form decline evident: struggling in scenarios where positioning previously sufficient (2 observations)
form_trajectory: negative: Consistent inability to match top sprinters' power and dropped on minimal climbs suggests declining physical capacity across both Grand Tour appearances
